page: Amazon Web Services c8gb.16xlarge is a compute-optimized server instance powered by the AWS Graviton processor family. It features 64 dedicated physical cores on the ARM64 architecture, utilizing the ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 model running at 2.8 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 128 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware.

Benchmark evaluations indicate strong multi-core capabilities and exceptional memory bandwidth. The server achieves top-tier status in multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, multi-core Geekbench scores, and software compilation tasks. Memory bandwidth tests show high throughput, with cached read speeds reaching 7387.52 GB/sec and uncached read speeds at 466.21 GB/sec. Additionally, the instance demonstrates high efficiency in database operations, Redis SET operations, and static web serving throughput. However, single-core performance and text processing benchmarks fall within the average range, and text generation for small-scale language models is a notable performance bottleneck.

Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 133 Gbps. The lack of local storage requires reliance on network-attached storage solutions, which represents a key architectural tradeoff. Given the high memory bandwidth and multi-core efficiency, this instance is suited for compute-bound workloads, software compilation, database operations, static web serving, and medium-to-large scale LLM text generation tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services c8gb.16xlarge is a compute-optimized server instance utilizing the ARM64 architecture. Powered by a 2.8 GHz AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor, it delivers 64 dedicated physical cores and 128 GB of RAM, establishing a 2.0 GB per core memory ratio. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, the instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators but features a 133 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark data highlights top-tier multi-core performance, exceptional memory bandwidth, and high efficiency in database operations, Redis SET transactions, and software compilation. Conversely, single-core processing and small-model LLM text generation represent relative performance bottlenecks. This server is designed for compute-intensive workloads, including high-throughput web serving, database management, and parallelized data processing.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8gb.16xlarge is an ARM64 compute-optimized server featuring 64 dedicated Graviton Neoverse-V2 cores, 128 GB RAM, 133 Gbps network bandwidth, and top-tier multi-core and memory performance.

tagline: An ARM64 compute-optimized server featuring sixty-four dedicated cores, high memory bandwidth, and high-speed network connectivity for parallel workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated ARM64 AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 physical cores at 2.8 GHz.
128 GB of system memory providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core.
133 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with Nitro hypervisor integration.
Top-tier memory bandwidth and multi-core processing benchmarks.
No local storage or GPU hardware included in the configuration.
Suited for software compilation, database operations, and web serving.
